title: phacelia glandulosa var. neomexicana
intro: Phacelia glandulosa var. neomexicana, also known as New Mexico phacelia, is a variety of the phacelia glandulosa plant, a flowering annual native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and supporting immune system health. Phacelia glandulosa var. neomexicana can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as used in salves and creams for topical applications.
